name: Number System
why: Foundation for almost every arithmetic OA question.
where: TCS NQT, Infosys, Wipro, product-company screening
formulas: Even/Odd: even +/- even = even; odd +/- odd = even; even +/- odd = odd.
Last digit cycles repeat for powers.
Remainder: (a+b) mod m = [(a mod m)+(b mod m)] mod m.
shortcuts: [Fast Trick] Before solving, estimate. If options are far apart, approximation is enough.
[Rookie Mistake] Do not start with long multiplication when percentage/fraction conversion is
possible.
[Pattern Spotting] Identify whether the question asks value, ratio, percent, or time.
patternRecognition: When you see number system questions, first underline given data, then mark the unknown. If answer options are numeric, estimate first. If ratios or percentages appear, convert to fractions where possible. Ye placement speed ka game hai - not only knowledge.

name: LCM & HCF
why: Fast handling of cycles, bells, remainders, and grouping.
where: TCS, Cognizant, Capgemini, banking prelims
formulas: a x b = LCM(a,b) x HCF(a,b).
LCM = highest powers of primes; HCF = lowest powers of common primes.
For bells together: answer = LCM of intervals.
shortcuts: [Fast Trick] Before solving, estimate. If options are far apart, approximation is enough.
[Rookie Mistake] Do not start with long multiplication when percentage/fraction conversion is
possible.
[Pattern Spotting] Identify whether the question asks value, ratio, percent, or time.
patternRecognition: When you see lcm & hcf questions, first underline given data, then mark the unknown. If answer options are numeric, estimate first. If ratios or percentages appear, convert to fractions where possible. Ye placement speed ka game hai - not only knowledge.

name: Divisibility

where: All service companies and campus tests
formulas: 3 or 9: digit sum rule.
11: difference of alternate digit sums is multiple of 11.
4: last two digits; 8: last three digits.
shortcuts: [Fast Trick] Before solving, estimate. If options are far apart, approximation is enough.
[Rookie Mistake] Do not start with long multiplication when percentage/fraction conversion is
possible.

patternRecognition: When you see divisibility questions, first underline given data, then mark the unknown. If answer options are numeric, estimate first. If ratios or percentages appear, convert to fractions where possible. Ye placement speed ka game hai - not only knowledge.

where: Every placement test
formulas: x% of y = y% of x.

name: Time & Work
why: Aptitude classic; often solved by LCM method.
where: TCS, Wipro, Cognizant, Accenture
formulas: Work = Efficiency x Time.
If A does in x days, one-day work = 1/x.
LCM method: take total work as LCM of times.
shortcuts: [LCM Trick] Take total work as LCM of days. It avoids fractions completely.
Yaha most students 1/x fractions me atak jaate hain. Use efficiency.
[Fast Trick] Before solving, estimate. If options are far apart, approximation is enough.
[Rookie Mistake] Do not start with long multiplication when percentage/fraction conversion is
possible.
[Pattern Spotting] Identify whether the question asks value, ratio, percent, or time.
patternRecognition: When you see time & work questions, first underline given data, then mark the unknown. If answer options are numeric, estimate first. If ratios or percentages appear, convert to fractions where possible. Ye placement speed ka game hai - not only knowledge.

name: Pipes & Cisterns

Time = Total capacity / Net rate.
shortcuts: [Fast Trick] Before solving, estimate. If options are far apart, approximation is enough.

name: Grammar
why: Grammar appears because companies test speed, clarity and pattern recognition.
where: Campus placements, internships, service-company OAs and startup hiring tests
formulas: Use elimination, diagramming, and keyword spotting.
Never assume extra information.
For verbal, choose the option that is grammatically correct and contextually natural.
shortcuts: [Fast Trick] Convert statements to symbols or a small table.
[Rookie Mistake] Avoid adding assumptions not present in the question.
[Pattern Spotting] Identify whether it is order, relation, grammar, or meaning.
patternRecognition: When you see grammar questions, first underline given data, then mark the unknown. If answer options are numeric, estimate first. If ratios or percentages appear, convert to fractions where possible. Ye placement speed ka game hai - not only knowledge.
